<|6.00|> none of this has anything to do with getting called to be on a team.<|11.00|>
<|11.00|> Baptism is not a field sport.<|15.00|>
<|15.00|> It costs you life, but it's not a sport.<|18.30|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And anyone who wants to hear their name called in baptism can.<|5.68|>
<|7.10|> Just let us know and we'll baptize you the next time that we do this.<|11.30|>
<|12.22|> We have 11 persons who can be baptized at this service and their names will be said throughout baptism again.<|22.30|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We will say the name of Indira and Margo and Miliana and Ezekiel and Isla and Adam and Kassara and Cassandra, Nachashia and Cyrus.<|16.32|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.98|> And after we call out their names,<|2.98|>
<|3.08|> the priest will say,<|4.10|>
<|4.36|> you are sealed by the Holy Spirit in baptism<|9.34|>
<|9.34|> and marked as Christ's own forever.<|13.38|>
<|15.36|> And if you were baptized years ago or decades ago,<|19.26|>
<|19.46|> these words can remind you of your own baptism.<|22.14|>
<|22.14|> And if you want to be baptized, let us go.<|23.80|>
<|24.14|> Let us know and we'll baptize you next time.<|27.20|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And all of these words, and all of these rituals, and this sacrament of the resurrection<|4.62|>
<|4.62|> is about recognition and belonging to the most essential dimensions of resurrection.<|14.06|>
<|15.40|> And through these words, and through these ancient rituals, may all of us, all of us,<|23.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> sense and hear the risen Christ<|3.50|>
<|3.50|> who knows us each by name<|5.98|>
